title: Heart Attack

steps.action: Call 112 immediately and keep the person seated with back support.
steps.warning: Do not delay emergency call even if pain seems to improve.

steps.action: Loosen tight clothing and keep the person calm with slow breathing.
steps.warning: Avoid walking or climbing stairs.

steps.action: If conscious and not allergic, chew one aspirin (300 mg) while waiting.
steps.warning: Skip aspirin if there is bleeding risk or known allergy.

doNot: Do not give food or heavy drinks.
Do not let the person drive.
Do not leave the person alone.

title: Choking

steps.action: Ask if they can cough or speak. Encourage forceful coughing if possible.
steps.warning: If no sound comes out, act immediately.

steps.action: Give 5 back blows between shoulder blades.
steps.warning: Support chest and lean the person forward.

steps.action: Give 5 abdominal thrusts and repeat cycles until object clears.
steps.warning: For pregnant or obese adults, use chest thrusts instead.

doNot: Do not perform blind finger sweeps.
Do not give water during active choking.

title: Snake Bite

steps.action: Move the person away from the snake and keep bitten limb still.
steps.warning: Movement spreads venom faster.

steps.action: Remove rings, bangles, or tight items near bite area.
steps.warning: Swelling can start quickly.

steps.action: Apply a clean, loose dressing and transport urgently.
steps.warning: Keep bite level with or slightly below heart.

doNot: Do not cut, suck, or ice the wound.
Do not apply tourniquet.
Do not try to catch the snake.

title: Severe Bleeding

steps.action: Wear gloves if available and press firmly with clean cloth.
steps.warning: Continuous direct pressure is the priority.

steps.action: If soaked, add more cloth without removing old layer.
steps.warning: Removing cloth may restart heavy bleeding.

steps.action: Raise injured part above heart level if no fracture is suspected.
steps.warning: Keep person lying down if dizzy.

doNot: Do not wash deep wounds.
Do not remove embedded objects.

title: Burns (Thermal)

steps.action: Cool burn under running cool water for 20 minutes.
steps.warning: Start as early as possible for better tissue protection.

steps.action: Cover with sterile non-stick dressing or clean cloth.
steps.warning: Keep dressing loose.

steps.action: Monitor for shock and pain while arranging medical help.
steps.warning: Large or facial burns need urgent care.

doNot: Do not use ice, toothpaste, butter, or powders.
Do not break blisters.

title: Suspected Fracture

steps.action: Keep the injured part still and support in current position.
steps.warning: Stabilize joints above and below the injury.

steps.action: Use a splint with padding if trained.
steps.warning: Splint should be snug, not tight.

steps.action: Apply cold pack wrapped in cloth for 10-15 minutes.
steps.warning: Avoid direct ice contact on skin.

doNot: Do not force bone back in place.
Do not allow weight-bearing on injured limb.

title: Seizure

steps.action: Clear nearby hard objects and cushion the head.
steps.warning: Protect from injury only.

steps.action: Turn person onto their side after jerking stops.
steps.warning: Side position helps airway protection.

steps.action: Time the seizure and observe breathing.
steps.warning: Any seizure over 5 minutes is an emergency.

doNot: Do not restrain movements.
Do not place anything in the mouth.

title: Drowning

steps.action: Ensure scene safety and bring person out of water if possible.
steps.warning: Do not endanger yourself.

steps.action: Check responsiveness and breathing; start CPR if absent.
steps.warning: Give rescue breaths early if trained.

steps.action: Keep warm and monitor until emergency team arrives.
steps.warning: Even recovered victims need medical evaluation.

doNot: Do not delay CPR waiting for water to drain.
Do not shake the person.

title: Electric Shock

steps.action: Switch off power source before touching the person.
steps.warning: Use dry non-conductive object if needed.

steps.action: Check breathing and pulse, start CPR if required.
steps.warning: Call emergency services immediately.

steps.action: Cover visible burns with sterile dressing.
steps.warning: Internal injury may be severe even with small skin burns.

doNot: Do not touch victim while current may still be active.
Do not use metal objects near source.

title: Heat Stroke

steps.action: Move person to shade or cool room immediately.
steps.warning: Heat stroke can cause organ damage quickly.

steps.action: Cool with wet cloth, fan, or cool water spray.
steps.warning: Target neck, armpits, and groin.

steps.action: If conscious, give small sips of cool water.
steps.warning: Stop fluids if confused or vomiting.

doNot: Do not give alcohol or caffeine.
Do not leave person in hot environment.

title: Hypothermia

steps.action: Move to warm, dry shelter and remove wet clothing.
steps.warning: Handle gently to avoid arrhythmias.

steps.action: Warm center of body first with blankets and body heat.
steps.warning: Warm chest, neck, and groin first.

steps.action: Give warm sweet drinks if fully alert.
steps.warning: No drinks if drowsy or confused.

doNot: Do not rub limbs aggressively.
Do not use very hot baths or heating pads directly.

title: Poisoning

steps.action: Identify substance and keep container or label nearby.
steps.warning: Share details with emergency responder.

steps.action: If inhaled, move to fresh air. If skin contact, rinse with water.
steps.warning: Protect yourself during rescue.

steps.action: Call poison control or emergency services immediately.
steps.warning: Symptoms may be delayed.

doNot: Do not induce vomiting unless advised by professionals.
Do not give random home remedies.

title: Asthma Attack

steps.action: Help person sit upright and use reliever inhaler with spacer.
steps.warning: Take one puff at a time with 4 breaths each.

steps.action: Repeat reliever doses every 4 minutes as needed.
steps.warning: Track total puffs and response.

steps.action: If no relief or severe breathlessness, call 112.
steps.warning: Blue lips or inability to speak is an emergency.

doNot: Do not let person lie flat.
Do not leave person alone during attack.

title: Severe Allergic Reaction (Anaphylaxis)

steps.action: Use epinephrine auto-injector immediately if available.
steps.warning: Inject into outer mid-thigh.

steps.action: Call 112 and lay person flat with legs raised.
steps.warning: If breathing is difficult, allow seated posture.

steps.action: Give second epinephrine dose after 5-10 minutes if needed.
steps.warning: Continue close breathing monitoring.

doNot: Do not rely only on antihistamines.
Do not delay epinephrine when symptoms are severe.

title: Stroke (FAST Signs)

steps.action: Check FAST: Face droop, Arm weakness, Speech difficulty, Time to call 112.
steps.warning: Note exact symptom start time.

steps.action: Keep person safe, seated, and calm while waiting.
steps.warning: Monitor breathing and consciousness.

steps.action: If unconscious and breathing, place in recovery position.
steps.warning: Prepare for CPR if breathing stops.

doNot: Do not give food, drink, or medicines.
Do not wait for symptoms to pass.
